key_points: Dorms cost significantly more than commuting for budget-conscious students
Bobcat Village mentioned positively by international exchange students
Financial trade-off debate is real — commuting saves thousands annually
key_quotes: Never ever live in dorms if you can possibly avoid it and you're not rich.
I used to live at bobcat village for one semester. I miss it so much!
I say dorm freshman year and if you decide you don't like it commute your second year.

key_points: Freshman dorm living seen as the primary path to making friends
Commuters worry about missing out on social integration
Campus described as lively with San Marcos offering real amenities
key_quotes: There are plenty of opportunities on campus to make friends and socialize.
San Marcos has everything even a hot topic... 30 minutes from Austin heck yeah.
By that point you've probably already made friends and have your community already.

key_points: No specific dining hall reviews in data — major gap in student feedback
Cost of food on campus is a real budget concern for students
Free food at home is cited as a genuine reason to commute
key_quotes: I would also need to pay for food as well, which is free at home.
Tuition alone costs about 12k... dorming will make the cost of attending increase a lot.

key_points: No direct mentions of campus mental health services in student data
Financial stress is a recurring and serious stressor for TXST students
Isolation concerns raised by commuter students about campus connection
key_quotes: I am taking the summer and fall to save up for rent/food/etc while I'm there.
Finances are your primary concern — commuting would be my recommendation.

key_points: Scholarship notification timeline unclear — students waiting with no answers
Even with $8k scholarship, students feel significant financial pressure
Out-of-state and transfer students face sticker shock on total costs
key_quotes: I'm curious to know if anyone else has received a scholarship offer — how long did it take?
Tuition alone ends up being 4k per year... dorming will make the cost increase a lot.
I am an International Student. [Only response to scholarship post, implying lack of local info]

key_points: Science & Engineering and audio/creative programs draw genuine interest
Transfer process from community college is an established pathway
No complaints about academic quality surfaced — neutral to positive
key_quotes: I've been working with a transfer advisor there for the past 5 months to ensure I'd have assured admission.
I plan to look into this university in hope to further study creative writing and animation.
I'm an audio student looking for a place to continue my audio training in the Austin area — this is the best option.

key_points: ApplyTexas portal bugs blocked multiple students from submitting applications
Support tickets go unanswered — students miss deadlines waiting for fixes
Transfer advisors praised individually, but system-level failures are real
key_quotes: I submitted multiple tickets to support with no emails back, and my application deadline passed.
I've submitted two emails to ApplyTexas' help desk. I got a response on the first saying they're looking into it but no instructions.
I called the school and they said it was an ApplyTexas issue — but that didn't help me submit.

key_points: Hilly campus with stairs and steep inclines — physically demanding to navigate
San Marcos location praised: affordable town, 30 minutes from Austin
Campus has grown dramatically — alumni from 1965–1982 barely recognize it
key_quotes: Thank you for the tour without having to walk up all those stairs and inclines.
San Marcos has everything... 30 minutes from Austin heck yeah.
It's hard to describe how much different the campus is now compared to how it was back in 1965.

key_points: Austin proximity is cited as a key career networking advantage
No direct data on job placement rates or employer recruiting presence
Engineering and creative arts students show career-focused intent in choosing TXST
key_quotes: 30 minutes from Austin heck yeah.
I plan to look into this university in hope to further study creative writing and animation.

key_points: In-state tuition relatively manageable but dorms erase savings quickly
Students actively strategizing to minimize costs — commuting, saving before enrolling
Scholarship amounts feel inadequate against total cost of attendance
key_quotes: Tuition alone costs about 12k and I got a scholarship for 8k per year... dorming will make the cost increase a lot.
I am taking the summer and fall to save up for rent/food/etc.
Never ever live in dorms if you can possibly avoid it and you're not rich.

key_points: Students choose TXST pragmatically — cost, location, program fit over prestige
Campus has genuine charm and alumni nostalgia is strong
Financial anxiety underlies most student decisions and discussions
key_quotes: This is the best option to be honest — San Marcos has everything.
Greetings from Germany. I love this campus. I have been an exchange student in 2013. I miss it so much!
Texas State is like they say you can be better, stronger, smarter than me but regardless I will always be best.

key_points: **Administration:** ApplyTexas bugs caused students to miss application deadlines with zero resolution
**Financial Aid:** Scholarship notification process is opaque with no clear timeline communicated
**Cost:** Total attendance cost shocks students post-admission despite manageable tuition headline
key_quotes: I submitted multiple tickets to support with no emails back, and my application deadline passed.
Similar problem! It won't let me submit my Texas residency. I tried multiple browsers and devices.
Tuition alone costs about 12k... dorming will make the cost of attending increase a lot.

key_points: San Marcos is genuinely affordable and walkable compared to Austin prices
Transfer advisor support individually praised — find a good one early
Audio and creative arts programs fly under the radar but draw dedicated students
key_quotes: San Marcos has everything even a hot topic — prefer this over a place like commerce and 30 minutes from Austin.
I've been working with a transfer advisor there for the past 5 months to ensure I'd have assured admission.
Greetings from Germany — I have been an exchange student in 2013. I miss it so much!
